name: Wiliwilinui
desc: Ridge community accessed via Waialae Iki — elevated homes, mature landscaping, trailhead to a spectacular ridge hike.
insider: Wiliwilinui Ridge Trail is one of the best hikes on Oahu — 4.8 miles round trip through native ohia forest to a Ko'olau summit with views of both coasts
You must check in with the security guard at the Waialae Iki gatehouse on Okoa Street to access the trailhead — arrive before 8am on weekends as parking passes are limited
The elevation (300-700 feet) delivers noticeably cooler temperatures and stronger breezes than the valley floors below — air conditioning is often unnecessary
The trail was reopened after years of closure and now requires a free permit from Na Ala Hele — book online in advance as they cap daily hikers
The community is small (roughly 150 homes) and accessed through Waialae Iki, giving it a double layer of seclusion from main road traffic
Mature Norfolk pines, monkeypod trees, and tropical landscaping give the neighborhood a lush, established feel that newer developments can't replicate
There are no commercial services whatsoever — you'll drive down to Kahala Mall or Hawaii Kai for groceries, dining, and everything else
The single access road through Waialae Iki means any road closure or accident blocks the only way home — it's the trade-off for total seclusion
Bird life along the ridge trail is remarkable — native forest birds including elepaio are spotted on the upper trail, and mejiro and red-crested cardinals are common in the neighborhood
Homes range from $1.5M to $3M+ and almost never appear on market — this is a place where families settle and stay for generations
